Details: Removal of 17a-ethynyl estradiol (EE2) by adsorptive media can be very effective and is dependent on the pH, the contaminant properties (pKa and hydrophobicity) and the surface charge of the media. One equilibrium sorption study showed no significant sorption of EE2 onto the positively charged alumina or the negatively charged silica; linear sorption was observed for EE2 onto Porapak, a hydrophobic organic media. The linear sorption coefficient to Porapak was 4.4E-3 L/m2.
